“Throughout the tragic circumstances of His trial and condemnation, Christ maintained a submissive demeanor even toward the chief priests and council who were plotting His death. These officers, however unworthy of their priestly power, were nevertheless in authority and had a certain measure of jurisdiction in secular as in ecclesiastical affairs. When He stood before Caiaphas, laden with insult…

When Paul was brought before the Jewish Council at Jerusalem, in order to divide his opponents he exclaimed : ‘I am a Pharisee, the son of a Pharisee: of the hope and resurrection of the dead I am called in question.’ And we read that the two parties in the Council proceeded to fall out on the instant, ‘for the Sadducees say that there is no resurrection, neither angel, nor spirit ; but the…

Jesus is your Savior. He created "worlds without number," and yet even in the midst of the vastness of His creations, Jesus cares for each one of you, as in these two illustrative episodes which involve audiences numbering only one. The resurrected Jesus visited Paul, incarcerated in a castle prison. Of that visit we read, And the night following the Lord stood by him, and said, Be of good cheer,…

Jesus is your Savior. He created "worlds without number," and yet even in the midst of the vastness of His creations, Jesus cares for each one of you, as in these two illustrative episodes which involve audiences numbering only one. The resurrected Jesus visited Paul, incarcerated in a castle prison. Of that visit we read, And the night following the Lord stood by him, and said, Be of good cheer,…

Jesus is your Savior. He created "worlds without number," and yet even in the midst of the vastness of His creations, Jesus cares for each one of you, as in these two illustrative episodes which involve audiences numbering only one. The resurrected Jesus visited Paul, incarcerated in a castle prison. Of that visit we read, And the night following the Lord stood by him, and said, Be of good cheer,…

By this saying, one might think that Paul made a mistake when he appealed to Caesar. After two years of imprisonment, Agrippa seems willing to let him go. Did Paul make a mistake in appealing to Caesar? It is doubtful that Agrippa really would have let him go. Besides, the Lord’s plan was to have his most vocal apostle testify before the greatest political authority on the earth. Paul had been…

conspiracy will be proven."

matches.lb: Musar: Shenei Luchot HaBerit, Torah Shebikhtav, Vayeshev, Miketz, Vayigash, Torah Ohr 47
matches.x: Another difficulty is that we have mentioned earlier is that the brothers had included the שכינה in their conspiracy not to reveal the sale of Joseph to anyone. If Jacob, who was flesh and blood, refused to be associated with the conspiracy of Shimon and Levi, as he said on his deathbed (Genesis 49,6), how could the שכינה have become a party to such a conspiracy?
CONSPIRACY OF THE JEWISH AUTHORITIES TO PUT JESUS TO DEATH—COMPACT WITH JUDAS.
Here, accused of traitorous conspiracy against his sovereign by king Saul, or by Cush one of his courtiers.
The "council" of the chief priests and elders is the Sanhedrin.
The "council" of the chief priests and elders is the Sanhedrin.
The "council" of the chief priests and elders is the Sanhedrin.

1 He was, of course, not yet king when he wrote this letter. ? See, on this, the notes in the commentary.

"He wrote one letter to his mother, but he didn't say where he was. That
is the last any of us heard from him."
Rehum =" compassion" a commander who wrote a letter to the king Artaxerxes in which he opposed the rebuilding of Jerusalem
